semanticCore: Valuation, price, negotiating, marking, cattle pasturing, or label
quranicRelevance: Pasturing cattle (Tuseemoon - 'feehi tuseemoon'), and marking/branding ('musawwameen', 'al-khayli al-musawwamati').

keyDerivations.meaning: To pasture cattle, let feed, graze, or release animals to fields
To mark, brand, label, design, make distinct, or launch marked units
Marked, branded, designated, or distinct (horses, stones, angels)

figurativeOrRareUsages: الْخَيْلِ الْمُسَوَّمَةِ (3:14): Branded/marked horses (the high aesthetic of premium pedigree horses).
حِجَارَةً مِنْ طِينٍ ۦ مُسَوَّمَةً عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ (51:33-34): Stones of clay, marked/designated by your Lord for the transgressors.
discardedNoise: The prophet Shem (Sam) son of Noah - genealogy folders.
summaryEn: The root connects pasturing (Iusamah) with marking/branding (Tasweem), detailing marked battle angels, branded pedigree horses, and marked clay stones of doom.
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر بين إرسال الماشية للمرعى (تُسيمون)، ووضع علامات التمييز (مسومة)، واصفاً الخيل المطهمة النبيلة، وملائكة بدر المسومة، وحجارة العذاب.
rawText: اسأمهُ 4 He, or it, made him to turn away with disgust, to be averse, to be disgusted, to loathe, or to nauseate. (M, K.) سَؤُومٌ an [intensive] epithet from 1 [meaning Wont, or much disposed, to turn away with disgust, to be averse, to be disgusted, to loathe, or to nauseate]: (S, M, K:) or much affected with vexation, or disgust; having little patience. (Ham p. 532.) سَئِمَ مِنْهُ 1 , (S, M, Msb, K,) and سَئِمَهُ, (M, Msb, K,) aor. ﹷ, (S, Msb, K,) inf. n. سَأَمٌ (S, M, K) and سَأْمٌ (K) and سَأَمَةٌ (S, M) and سَآمٌ and سَآمَةٌ, (S, Msb, K,) He turned away with disgust from it; was averse from it; was disgusted at it or with it; loathed, or nauseated, it; (S, M, Msb, K;) namely, a thing; (S, M, K;) syn. مَلَّ, (S, M, Msb, K,) and ضَجِرَ: (Msb:) سَآمَةٌ exceeds مَلَالٌ. (Ham pp. 775-6.) It is said in the Kur [xli. 49], لَا يَسْأٓمُ ٱلْإِنْسَانُ مِنْ دُعَآءِ ٱلْخَيْرِ [Man will not turn away with disgust from, or will not be weary of, praying for good, or wealth, or prosperity]. (Msb.) And in a trad., إِنَّ ٱللّٰهَ لَا يَسْأَمُ حَتَّى تَسْأَمُوا Verily God will not turn away with disgust until ye turn away with disgust; like لَا يَمَلُّ حَتَّى تَمَلُّوا, and thus the trad. is commonly related. (TA.) And it is related in a trad. of 'Áïsheh that she used to say to the Jews, عَلَيْكُمْ السَّأْمُ وَالذَّأْمُ وَاللَّعْنَةُ [Disgust, or loathing, and contempt, and cursing, rest upon you]: thus related with ء, meaning ye shall turn away with disgust from your religion: but commonly related without ء [and with a different meaning], as will be stated hereafter [in art. سوم]. (IAth, TA in this art. and in art. سوم.)
